description: 76 out-of-domain scenarios written to expose weaknesses of the system: claims not present in the training corpora, uncomfortable truths, and plausible-sounding hoaxes. Labels: FAKE / REAL. Each scenario carries `length` (short: single claim sentence; long: ~100-260 word article-style paragraph) and, for FAKE items, `style`: `human_typical` (classic disinformation tropes - secret, leaked, whistleblower, anonymous source) or `ai_fluent` (fluent, source-attributed prose with none of those tropes). `ai_fluent` scenarios additionally carry `provenance`: `external_dataset` (18 scenarios) are ChatGPT-3.5 paraphrases/rewrites of real human-written misinformation, drawn unbiasedly (fixed random seed, stratified by domain and generation method, no cherry-picking) from Chen & Shu's LLMFake dataset (ICLR 2024, github.com/llm-misinformation/llm-misinformation), PolitiFact/GossipCop subset only (the CoAID subset was excluded after a text-overlap check found it leaks into this project's own COVID training data). `hand_authored` (8 scenarios, all short) remain hand-written: no clean public short-claim LLM-paraphrase corpus was available (CoAID would have fit but is contaminated). REAL items are tagged `style: reporting`. See README 'AI-generated disinformation is harder to detect' for the measured comparison.

scenarios.label: FAKE
scenarios.text: Pope Francis officially endorsed Donald Trump for president in 2016.

scenarios.label: REAL
scenarios.text: The U.S. Senate voted 68 to 29 on Thursday to confirm the nomination, ending a confirmation process that had stretched over four months amid disputes on the Judiciary Committee. Supporters argued the nominee's record on antitrust enforcement made her a natural fit for the role, while opponents raised concerns about her past rulings on regulatory authority. The vote followed a similar pattern to previous confirmations this term, with most Republicans opposing the nomination and a handful of moderates from both parties crossing party lines. Analysts noted that the confirmation gives the administration its fourth appointment to the agency's leadership since taking office, a pace consistent with recent administrations. The nominee is expected to be sworn in within the next two weeks and will immediately join ongoing deliberations on several pending enforcement cases. In a statement released after the vote, the nominee thanked lawmakers on both sides of the aisle and pledged to enforce the law "without fear or favor." The White House called the outcome a validation of its broader personnel strategy for the agency.

scenarios.label: REAL
scenarios.text: Consumer prices rose 0.3% in November, in line with economists' expectations, as energy costs eased for a second consecutive month, the Bureau of Labor Statistics reported Tuesday. On an annual basis, inflation slowed to 3.1%, down from 3.4% in October, driven largely by falling gasoline prices and a moderation in shelter costs, which still account for the largest share of the index. Core inflation, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, held steady at 3.8% year-over-year, slightly above the Federal Reserve's long-term target. Policymakers have signaled they want to see several more months of consistent data before considering further changes to interest rates. Retailers reported steady holiday spending despite the higher price levels, with online sales up 6% compared with the same period last year. Economists at several major banks said the data supports a "soft landing" scenario in which inflation continues to cool without triggering a sharp rise in unemployment. The next inflation report is due in early January, ahead of the central bank's first policy meeting of the year.

scenarios.label: REAL
scenarios.text: A team of researchers at the Max Planck Institute published findings this week describing a new method for detecting gravitational lensing effects in distant galaxy clusters, using a machine-learning model trained on more than two million telescope images. The technique, described in a paper in Nature Astronomy, identifies subtle distortions in light caused by the gravity of massive objects far more quickly than earlier manual methods, which could take specialists weeks to review a single cluster. The team validated the model against known lensing events cataloged by the Hubble and James Webb telescopes and reported an accuracy of roughly 94% in identifying genuine lensing arcs. Independent astronomers not involved in the study said the approach could meaningfully speed up the search for dark matter distribution maps once applied to data from the upcoming Vera C. Rubin Observatory, which is expected to generate far more images than researchers can review manually. The authors cautioned that the model still requires manual confirmation for borderline cases and said they plan to release the training pipeline as open-source software later this year.

scenarios.label: REAL
scenarios.text: Several major airlines announced this week that they will begin offering real-time turbulence forecasting to pilots using a shared data network that pools anonymized flight telemetry across carriers. The system, developed with input from the National Weather Service, cross-references altitude, speed and vibration data from thousands of flights to flag turbulence pockets that traditional radar can miss, particularly clear-air turbulence at cruising altitude. Airlines participating in the pilot program said early results suggest a meaningful reduction in unexpected in-flight incidents, though full statistics will not be published until the program completes its first full year. Passenger advocacy groups welcomed the move, noting that turbulence-related injuries, while rare, have risen in recent years as climate change is linked to more frequent clear-air turbulence at commercial cruising altitudes. Regulators said they are monitoring the rollout closely and may consider requiring similar systems industry-wide if the data proves reliable. The program is expected to expand to additional carriers by the end of next year.

scenarios.label: REAL
scenarios.text: A major cloud computing provider announced Tuesday that it has completed the first phase of migrating its European data centers to run primarily on renewable energy sources, a project the company says has been underway since 2019. The announcement covers twelve facilities across Ireland, Germany and the Netherlands, which the company says now draw the majority of their power from wind and solar contracts signed directly with regional utilities. Independent energy analysts said the move reflects a broader trend among large technology firms to lock in long-term renewable contracts as a hedge against volatile energy prices, rather than purely as an environmental commitment. The company's sustainability report, published alongside the announcement, also disclosed that overall water usage for cooling across its European operations declined for the third straight year, attributing the drop to newer, more efficient cooling designs. Competing cloud providers have made similar pledges in recent years, though the pace of actual implementation has varied widely and remains difficult for outside observers to verify independently. The company said it expects to publish updated figures for its next reporting cycle in the spring.

scenarios.label: REAL
scenarios.text: Health authorities in several countries updated their COVID-19 booster recommendations this fall to target more recent variants in circulation, following guidance from the World Health Organization's vaccine composition advisory group. The updated shots are formulated to better match strains that have been dominant since early in the year, rather than the original 2020 strain used in the first generation of vaccines. Officials emphasized that the update follows the same seasonal approach used for influenza vaccines, which are reformulated annually based on circulating strains rather than because earlier versions have failed. Clinical data reviewed by regulators showed the updated boosters produced a stronger antibody response against currently circulating variants compared with the prior formulation, though officials cautioned that real-world effectiveness data will continue to be collected over the coming months. Uptake of the updated boosters has varied significantly by country, with several public health agencies launching new outreach campaigns ahead of the winter respiratory illness season. Pharmacies in most participating countries began offering the updated shots alongside seasonal flu vaccines.

scenarios.label: FAKE
scenarios.text: According to several anonymous congressional staffers, a secret addendum to last year's budget bill diverts nearly $40 billion to a classified program that political insiders describe only as "the continuity initiative." None of the lawmakers who allegedly voted for the addendum will confirm its existence on the record, and a leaked internal memo — which several fact-checking sites have dismissed as fabricated — claims the funding bypasses normal oversight committees entirely. Whistleblowers say the program has existed in some form since the 1990s, hidden inside classified riders that few members of Congress ever actually read before voting. Critics argue this is exactly how the political establishment keeps the public in the dark about where trillions of tax dollars really go every year. One self-described former Pentagon contractor, speaking only on condition of anonymity, claimed to have seen internal documents referencing the program by a classified codename, though no such documents have ever been independently verified or made public. Despite repeated requests for comment, congressional offices have either denied the program's existence or declined to respond at all — which supporters of the theory say only proves how deep the cover-up really goes.

scenarios.label: FAKE
scenarios.text: A viral post circulating on social media claims that leaked internal emails from a major vaccine manufacturer prove the company knew about severe, undisclosed side effects years before its COVID-19 vaccine was approved. The post, shared tens of thousands of times, alleges that regulators were secretly pressured to fast-track approval despite internal warnings from company scientists who were reportedly silenced or forced to sign non-disclosure agreements. No verified copies of the alleged emails have surfaced, and several fact-checking organizations have found no evidence the documents are genuine, but supporters insist mainstream outlets are refusing to cover the story because of undisclosed financial ties to pharmaceutical advertisers. Some versions of the post go further, claiming that hospitals were secretly instructed to misclassify vaccine injuries as unrelated conditions to keep official statistics artificially low. Several anonymous accounts claiming to be former hospital employees have echoed the allegations online, though none have provided documentation or agreed to be identified. The claim continues to circulate widely despite repeated debunking, with new variations of the story appearing every few months under different framing.

scenarios.label: FAKE
scenarios.text: A widely shared video claims to show 'proof' that a major social media platform secretly throttles the reach of posts that criticize its advertisers, using an internal ranking system insiders have nicknamed 'the dampener.' The video, narrated by an anonymous former employee whose face is blurred throughout, alleges that the system was built specifically to protect major ad revenue relationships and has never been disclosed to regulators or the public. No source code, internal documents, or verifiable evidence accompanies the claims beyond the narrator's testimony, and the platform has denied the allegations in a brief statement. Supporters of the claim argue that the total absence of hard evidence is itself suspicious, insisting that a company capable of hiding a global surveillance-scale ranking system would obviously also be capable of hiding the evidence of it. The video has been re-uploaded under dozens of different titles after each original upload was removed, which proponents cite as further proof of an active cover-up rather than routine content moderation. Several tech journalists who reviewed the video's claims said they could not verify a single specific technical detail described in it.

scenarios.label: FAKE
scenarios.text: An anonymously authored report circulating in online trading forums claims that a coordinated group of hedge funds secretly agreed to short a mid-size regional bank's stock in order to trigger a liquidity crisis and buy up its assets at a steep discount. The report cites unnamed 'sources close to the matter' and a supposedly leaked group chat transcript, though no verified copies of the transcript have been produced and the bank in question has stated it saw no unusual trading patterns beyond normal market volatility. Proponents of the theory argue that regulators have deliberately avoided investigating the claims because several commissioners have undisclosed personal ties to the funds allegedly involved. The theory has picked up traction after a well-known finance commentator shared it without independent verification, adding that 'this is exactly how they always do it' and urging followers to withdraw deposits as a precaution. Several economists who reviewed the publicly available trading data said the described pattern is indistinguishable from ordinary short-selling activity that occurs constantly in volatile markets, but the claim continues to circulate, gaining new anonymous 'confirmations' each week.

scenarios.label: FAKE
scenarios.source: Chen, C. & Shu, K. (2024). Can LLM-Generated Misinformation Be Detected? ICLR 2024. LLMFake dataset (github.com/llm-misinformation/llm-misinformation), ChatGPT-3.5 paraphrase of a human-written misinformation claim, PolitiFact/GossipCop subset (CoAID subset excluded: confirmed text overlap with this project's own COVID training data). news_id=politifact14085
scenarios.text: According to reports, Bill O'Reilly was approached by two women and three men at a bagel shop in New York City who expressed their disgust towards his alleged behavior towards women at Fox News. After being told to leave him alone, one of the men grabbed O'Reilly and he was then violently attacked by the other two men until he was rendered unconscious and bleeding. The attackers fled the scene and the police arrived within minutes, taking O'Reilly to St. Vincent's hospital where he is in critical condition with injuries that may be life-threatening. The family is asking for privacy and the police are seeking witnesses or evidence of the attack. Some liberals have praised the attack as deserved, which highlights the hypocrisy of their beliefs against cruel and unusual punishment.

scenarios.label: FAKE
scenarios.source: Chen, C. & Shu, K. (2024). Can LLM-Generated Misinformation Be Detected? ICLR 2024. LLMFake dataset (github.com/llm-misinformation/llm-misinformation), ChatGPT-3.5 paraphrase of a human-written misinformation claim, PolitiFact/GossipCop subset (CoAID subset excluded: confirmed text overlap with this project's own COVID training data). news_id=politifact14699
scenarios.text: Michigan DNR responded to an urgent call reporting a giant squid that had landed on Leland. Marine Biologist Dr. Debra Polowotski examined the specimen, which happened to be a freshwater species of the Architeuthis genus, confirming its rarity. The squid's presence in Lake Michigan came as a surprise as authorities were under the impression that the Cleveland Sea World complex contained all the squids. However, it was discovered that some had escaped and made their way to Lake Erie. The squids were contained in Lake Erie as they require vast spaces and are intimidated by closed-in areas. It was revealed that the discovered squid is a female and had laid eggs. Dr. Polowotski warns that exercise caution while swimming or boating as the walleye may not control the small population of these squids.

scenarios.label: FAKE
scenarios.source: Chen, C. & Shu, K. (2024). Can LLM-Generated Misinformation Be Detected? ICLR 2024. LLMFake dataset (github.com/llm-misinformation/llm-misinformation), ChatGPT-3.5 paraphrase of a human-written misinformation claim, PolitiFact/GossipCop subset (CoAID subset excluded: confirmed text overlap with this project's own COVID training data). news_id=politifact13942
scenarios.text: The government has put in place a new rule stating that all motorcycles must have a governor fitted by mid-January 2017 to prevent them from exceeding 75 miles per hour. This regulation aims to reduce motorcycle fatalities in the US, with many politicians having long pushed for stricter safety measures. Major motorcycle manufacturers were offered a large tax break by the government in 2014 to fit governors on all new motorcycles, but they declined. The US Department of Transportation played a key role in implementing the new regulation and has emphasized the need for it, saying that the majority of motorcycle fatalities are due to unsafe speeds. Exceptions have been made for states allowing higher speeds, and designated shops will offer subsidies for retrofitting motorcycles with the speed governor. Failure to comply with the new rule may result in fines for both riders and manufacturers.

scenarios.label: FAKE
scenarios.source: Chen, C. & Shu, K. (2024). Can LLM-Generated Misinformation Be Detected? ICLR 2024. LLMFake dataset (github.com/llm-misinformation/llm-misinformation), ChatGPT-3.5 paraphrase of a human-written misinformation claim, PolitiFact/GossipCop subset (CoAID subset excluded: confirmed text overlap with this project's own COVID training data). news_id=gossipcop-3967904178
scenarios.text: Tune in to Entertainment Tonight on Yahoo View. There have always been rumors and speculation surrounding the relationship of Gwen Stefani and Blake Shelton. This time, it is being reported by In Touch Weekly that Shelton ended things with Stefani in February before going on tour. However, recent photos posted by Stefani on Instagram show the two appearing quite affectionate on stage. These photos are not the first instances of Stefani sharing pictures since February. She also posted a jumbotron kiss and pictures of herself with other people backstage. She has also publicly supported Shelton through social media, even wishing him well when he was sick. According to an insider, the rumors of the breakup are false, but Stefani's social media behavior is seen as a desperate attempt to ignore the truth. It is speculated that Shelton ended things due to Stefani's neediness, drama, and jealousy. Reasons for this include her critical ex-husband and her discomfort with Shelton's relationship with other women, including voice champion Chloe Kohanski and his ex-wife Miranda Lambert. While Shelton does not reciprocate Stefani's social media gestures, he does have a picture of her as his profile photo. For more updates, visit Yahoo Entertainment.

scenarios.label: FAKE
scenarios.source: Chen, C. & Shu, K. (2024). Can LLM-Generated Misinformation Be Detected? ICLR 2024. LLMFake dataset (github.com/llm-misinformation/llm-misinformation), ChatGPT-3.5 paraphrase of a human-written misinformation claim, PolitiFact/GossipCop subset (CoAID subset excluded: confirmed text overlap with this project's own COVID training data). news_id=gossipcop-1369753636
scenarios.text: Megyn shouldn't be too concerned about the National Enquirer, as they have supported absurd claims in the past. Does anyone actually take this publication seriously? It's clear that Trump's allies at the National Enquirer are once again defending him. The New York Times and Washington Post are also guilty of biased reporting, but the National Enquirer at least knows that few people believe its stories. Other publications, on the other hand, pretend to be legitimate journalists. Chelsea Clinton is trying to bond with Megyn over this issue, but it's not appropriate. It's unlikely that they will become friends because of it. Some people are suggesting that the National Enquirer will create false reports about Megyn, including a scandalous lesbian affair. However, she is not a Clinton, so this is unlikely to happen. It's interesting to see how some of Chelsea's supporters are being mean to Megyn because of her past support for Trump. They seem to hold a grudge against her. Despite all of this, Megyn shouldn't feel sorry for herself. She has made controversial statements in the past and some people don't like her because of it. Chelsea may have hoped for a love fest in bonding over the National Enquirer, but her followers are being too mean to Megyn. Clearly, Chelsea didn't anticipate this response, but we did.

scenarios.label: FAKE
scenarios.source: Chen, C. & Shu, K. (2024). Can LLM-Generated Misinformation Be Detected? ICLR 2024. LLMFake dataset (github.com/llm-misinformation/llm-misinformation), ChatGPT-3.5 paraphrase of a human-written misinformation claim, PolitiFact/GossipCop subset (CoAID subset excluded: confirmed text overlap with this project's own COVID training data). news_id=gossipcop-8511922365
scenarios.text: According to a new report, Angelina Jolie might be getting married again. She has supposedly been dating a mysterious man for several months without telling her ex-husband Brad Pitt. Although she is still legally married to Brad, Angelina is allegedly planning her fourth wedding to a wealthy businessman in his forties. The couple began dating in early 2017 and have been keeping their relationship very secret. Angelina has chosen not to tell Brad about her new relationship and hopes to surprise him. She has also not shared the news with her children, fearing that they will inform their father. The wedding will reportedly take place in Cambodia, Angelina's second home, and will be a traditional Buddhist ceremony. The couple plans to wear traditional Cambodian attire and serve local dishes at the reception. Angelina's closest friends and family will be in attendance, and her children will have roles in the wedding. It is unclear how Brad will react when he learns about Angelina's plans.

scenarios.label: FAKE
scenarios.source: Chen, C. & Shu, K. (2024). Can LLM-Generated Misinformation Be Detected? ICLR 2024. LLMFake dataset (github.com/llm-misinformation/llm-misinformation), ChatGPT-3.5 paraphrase generation of a human-written misinformation claim, PolitiFact/GossipCop subset (CoAID subset excluded: confirmed text overlap with this project's own COVID training data). news_id=gossipcop-3414394050
scenarios.text: Broadway star Jennifer Holliday has withdrawn from performing at the upcoming presidential inaugural due to backlash from her fans, particularly from the LGBTQ community. Holliday expressed regret for her earlier decision and apologized for causing any disappointment. This withdrawal follows the pattern of several other prominent entertainers who have declined to perform at the inauguration. Despite this, country star Toby Keith and actor Jon Voigt are still expected to participate in the concert. Holliday's decision was confirmed by her representative, who shared a letter she wrote to her fans in which she emphasized her support for the LGBTQ community. In a previous interview, Holliday defended her initial decision, explaining that she saw it as performing for the people in attendance rather than a political statement. However, the negative response she received, including death threats and calls for boycotts, deeply affected her. Holliday was scheduled to perform at the "Make America Great Again! Welcome Celebration" alongside Keith, Voigt, and President-elect Donald Trump. Compared to Barack Obama's inaugurations, Trump's event does not feature as many high-profile celebrities. Trump has stated that he prefers the focus to be on the people rather than the presence of A-list stars.

scenarios.label: FAKE
scenarios.source: Chen, C. & Shu, K. (2024). Can LLM-Generated Misinformation Be Detected? ICLR 2024. LLMFake dataset (github.com/llm-misinformation/llm-misinformation), ChatGPT-3.5 paraphrase generation of a human-written misinformation claim, PolitiFact/GossipCop subset (CoAID subset excluded: confirmed text overlap with this project's own COVID training data). news_id=gossipcop-5543072612
scenarios.text: Drew Barrymore, who has expressed her unhappiness with her dating life, has found a pleasant distraction in Kristen Stewart, according to exclusive information obtained by RadarOnline.com. Both actresses, who have openly discussed their bisexuality, have developed a close bond. Stewart, who is currently in discussions for a reboot of Charlie's Angels, has sought guidance from Barrymore, who starred in and produced the 2000 film and its sequel. The insider reveals that there is noticeable chemistry between the two, with both actresses displaying flirtatious behavior. Barrymore has unofficially joined the project as a mentor and her lively personality is helping Stewart, who can be reserved, to come out of her shell. Interestingly, when Barrymore is present, Stewart never mentions her girlfriend Stella Maxwell, leaving room for speculation about what might happen during filming. If you have any interesting information, please contact RadarOnline.com at tips@radaronline.com or call 800-344-9598.

scenarios.label: FAKE
scenarios.source: Chen, C. & Shu, K. (2024). Can LLM-Generated Misinformation Be Detected? ICLR 2024. LLMFake dataset (github.com/llm-misinformation/llm-misinformation), ChatGPT-3.5 paraphrase generation of a human-written misinformation claim, PolitiFact/GossipCop subset (CoAID subset excluded: confirmed text overlap with this project's own COVID training data). news_id=gossipcop-1289487505
scenarios.text: Following his rise to fame in Twilight, Robert Pattinson has become increasingly private due to the negative risk and reward balance that comes with opening up in interviews. The actor, who is currently dating singer F.K.A. Twigs, prefers to keep his personal life under wraps to avoid being labeled as dishonest or having his personality misconstrued. He has even gone to great lengths to maintain his privacy, including changing cars and outfits to confuse paparazzi and going as far as hiding in trunks to escape their attention. Pattinson admits that disappearing is not difficult, but it requires effort that most people are unwilling to put in.

scenarios.label: FAKE
scenarios.source: Chen, C. & Shu, K. (2024). Can LLM-Generated Misinformation Be Detected? ICLR 2024. LLMFake dataset (github.com/llm-misinformation/llm-misinformation), ChatGPT-3.5 rewrite generation of a human-written misinformation claim, PolitiFact/GossipCop subset (CoAID subset excluded: confirmed text overlap with this project's own COVID training data). news_id=gossipcop-4047356713
scenarios.text: The Duke and Duchess of Cambridge and Prince Harry recently participated in a London Marathon training day, showcasing their commitment to fitness. During the event, observers noticed a concerning change in Kate Middleton's appearance, as her once well-fitting leggings seemed loose on her shrinking frame. Reports have surfaced suggesting that the princess is battling an eating disorder, compulsively inducing vomiting after every meal. It appears that Kate's struggle to hide this shocking secret is becoming more difficult, evident in her frail physique. Photographs have emerged, revealing the telltale signs of her vomiting habits, such as Band-Aids wrapped around her fingers and thumbs, commonly associated with individuals suffering from bulimia. Concerns for Kate's well-being have heightened, with comparisons drawn to her late mother-in-law, Princess Diana, who also privately battled bulimia. Mental health experts have noted the significance of these signs on her hands and stress the detrimental effects of such weight-obsessed behaviors. Although Kate sought assistance from a prominent British medical expert for the early stages of anorexia a few years ago, insiders reveal that further treatment may be necessary. A doctor has recommended residential treatment to address her eating disorder. It is crucial for Kate to prioritize her health and well-being amidst the pressure she faces as a member of the royal family.

scenarios.label: FAKE
scenarios.source: Chen, C. & Shu, K. (2024). Can LLM-Generated Misinformation Be Detected? ICLR 2024. scenarios.text: Joe Jonas enjoyed some sunshine in Australia along with a group of female companions, though Sophie Turner was notably absent. It appears that the singer was simply engaging with enthusiastic fans, who were eager to take photos with him on Bondi Beach in Sydney on February 22. Joe, who is currently in Australia filming the upcoming season of The Voice Australia, took the opportunity to connect with his admirers while his fiancée took care of her own commitments. Prior to his trip, the couple had spent quality time together on a secluded island in the Maldives and celebrated Sophie's 22nd birthday in New York City on February 21. Joe's brother, Nick Jonas, even expressed his fondness for Sophie on social media, proving her warmth and acceptance within the family. It is evident that Sophie Turner has no issue with Joe being photographed with other women, as evidenced by her birthday video tribute on Instagram. In it, she can be seen enjoying Taylor Swift's song "22," a cheeky reference to Joe's past relationship with the pop star. It is clear that any lingering history between the two former flames has no bearing on Sophie's understanding and confidence in their current relationship. Rest assured, HollywoodLifers, while Sophie may not be in Australia with Joe, their bond remains strong.

scenarios.text: Anna Wintour, the current Editor in Chief of Vogue magazine, holds an esteemed position within the realm of magazine publishing, complemented by her noteworthy net worth of $35 million. Her journey commenced within the fashion industry and later transitioned into the field of journalism, fueled in part by her parents' encouragement. In 1970, Wintour commenced her career as an editorial assistant for the newly launched Harper's & Queen fashion magazine. Even at that nascent stage, her aspirations of editing Vogue were evident, eventually fulfilled almost two decades later. Wintour, from her inaugural issue onwards, swiftly implemented significant transformations within the pages of Vogue. Notably, these modifications were apparent right on the magazine's cover. Departing from the magazine's previous preference for tightly framed headshots, Wintour favored a more naturalistic style of photography, showcasing lesser-known models in outdoor settings. Moreover, Wintour's debut Vogue cover shattered conventions as it featured a model clad in jeans, the first of its kind, accentuated by a lavishly adorned T-shirt valued at approximately $10,000. Under Wintour's leadership, Vogue has remained a pivotal pillar within the realm of fashion journalism, maintaining its influential stance without wavering.

scenarios.text: Brian Kline, who earns $11.66 an hour working in retail and is being treated for cancer thanks to Medicaid, asked Health and Human Services Secretary Tom Price why he wanted to take away his Medicaid expansion at a CNN town hall. Price responded that the goal was to ensure that every American has access to care and coverage they want, but that current healthcare costs were unsustainable and that tough choices needed to be made. Those choices, he argued, included cutting Medicaid benefits or taking out more loans from countries like China. While he understood that such an approach sound insensitive, it was necessary to confront the practical reality facing the nation.

scenarios.text: It has been revealed that a woman who claimed that Roy Moore pursued her when she was a teenager is a strong supporter of the Democratic Party and has worked for the DNC. Deborah Wesson Gibson, also known as Debbie Gibson, has interpreted for campaigns for Joe Biden and Hillary Clinton, and has expressed her support for Doug Jones in the Alabama Senate race. In addition, she strongly opposes Roy Moore as evidenced by her social media activity. The Moore campaign alleges that the Washington Post, which endorsed Jones, has been bribing people to find negative information about Moore. Moore himself claims that this is a smear campaign orchestrated by Democrats who hate him for his biblical morality, and urges supporters to pray for him and donate to his campaign.

scenarios.text: US President Donald Trump has signed an executive order which now allows all Ghana nationals to travel to the US without visas for Tourism or Business purposes only, staying a maximum of 180 days. However, if Ghanaians hold dual nationality including a passport from Syria, Iraq, Iran, Sudan, Libya, Somalia or Yemen, they will be banned from entering the US. The order is aimed at strengthening trade relations between the two countries. Executive orders have also been signed regarding environmental concerns and high-priority infrastructure projects. Trump also signed an executive order on Monday barring federal funds from international organizations that promote abortion. He withdrew the United States from the Trans-Pacific Partnership trade deal on Monday.

scenarios.text: Los Angeles prosecutors have charged Bumble Bee Foods and two of its managers with violating safety regulations in the death of a worker who was killed by being inadvertently placed in an industrial oven with tons of canned tuna. Jose Melena was reportedly performing maintenance in a 35-foot-long oven at the company's Santa Fe Springs plant when a worker mistakenly filled the pressure cooker with 12,000 pounds of tuna and turned it on, upon mistakenly believing Melena was in the bathroom. Upon realising that Melena was missing, a search ensued during which Melena's body was found in oven, after it had been turned off and opened. The company, its plant operations director, Angel Rodriguez and former safety manager, Saul Florez, have all been charged with three counts of violating Occupational Safety & Health Administration rules that caused a death. Rodriguez and Florez could face up to three years in prison and fines of up to $250,000 if they are convicted, whilst Bumble Bee Foods faces a maximum fine of $1.5m.

scenarios.text: Broward County Sheriff Scott Israel has been dismissed over claims of a cover-up in relation to the Parkland school shooting. The decision to remove Sheriff Israel was made following a criminal investigation by Florida Governor Rick Scott. Reports suggest that Sheriff Israel could be replaced as early as next week. Sources reveal that Sheriff Israel is being removed for suspected criminal offences, rather than malfeasance, and that he is also reportedly being investigated by the IRS. Despite his public criticism of FBI Director Christopher Wray following the Parkland shooting, Sheriff Israel's own handling of the tragedy has been called into question. Police officers, under his instructions, were called to the home of the Parkland shooter 45 times prior to the school shooting. Sheriff Israel's removal appears to be linked to a criminal matter that the FBI is aware of, although it is unclear why law enforcement officials failed to investigate the Parkland shooter properly. Nevertheless, their failure has been attributed to the loss of 17 lives. In addition to his handling of the Parkland shooting, Sheriff Israel has been heavily criticised for his close association with Democratic politicians like Hillary Clinton and Debbie Wasserman Schultz, which has led some to speculate that his political views may have influenced his actions as sheriff. Other controversies relating to the sheriff's department include accusations of a cover-up over an alleged assault by Sheriff Israel's son, Brett, on a 14 year old student on campus, and a controversial tweet from the Broward County Sheriff's office about a custom made Broward sheriff's car.

scenarios.text: Tragic events of a house explosion in New Jersey must be clarified: there is no evidence of foul play. The couple who perished in the explosion, John Paladino and Carole Paladino, were longtime residents of the area. However, there have been unsubstantiated claims linking Carole Paladino's potential testimony against the Clinton Foundation to the explosion. The expected testimony concerned a pharmaceutical company that had donated to the Clinton Foundation, allegedly in an effort to cover up their drug price gouging crimes. This claim is part of a larger narrative that suggests there is a "Clinton body count". It should be noted that there is no factual basis for such claims and they should be viewed as unfounded conspiracy theories. Regardless, this tragedy has brought attention to the important issue of medical scams in the US, such as EpiPen price gouging, and the devastating impact of the opioid crisis.
